chief_complaint: A 66-year-old male patient presented to the outpatient clinic with worsening dyspnea.
history: The patient had a conduction system pacing (CSP) pacemaker, implanted after a pace-and-ablate strategy for persistent, poorly rate-controlled atrial fibrillation. He presented with worsening dyspnea.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Laboratory findings and ECG were indicative of an anterolateral ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI). Coronary angiography showed occlusion of a large diagonal branch.
